name: Cinematic Luxury
summary: Dramatic black-canvas premium system with full-bleed hero pacing, restrained metallic/red accents, and monumental display type.

style_dna.philosophy: Premium restraint: fewer elements, stronger contrast, more intentional silence.
style_dna.visual_signature: Near-black canvas, large white display type, cinematic imagery, and one scarce premium accent.
style_dna.surface_logic: Black hero, elevated dark panels, occasional light editorial insert.

palette.accent_policy: One accent moment per slide, preferably as a rule, number, or small CTA.
palette.semantic_policy: Avoid generic status colors; premium systems use fewer signals.

layout.grid: Hero-first layouts, asymmetry, oversized type, large negative space.
layout.whitespace: Sparse and theatrical; do not fill the black canvas.
layout.image_treatment: Full-bleed cinematic photography, hard crops, no stock-like blur.

components.cards: Dark panels, thin borders, no soft SaaS feel.
components.tables: Spec grids with large numbers and sparse rows.
components.callouts: Monumental numbers, chapter bands, and image-caption pairs.

slides.accent_rule: Use gold/metal accent sparingly against black canvas.

document.heading_rule: Documents become premium editorial on white paper, not black Word pages unless PDF-only.

source_inheritance.method: Synthesized from local getdesign.md working corpus.

do: Let black canvas and image crops create drama.
dont: Do not fill the page with cards.
Do not use friendly SaaS pills everywhere.
Do not brighten the palette unnecessarily.

pptx: Use deterministic HTML/CSS or PPTX text boxes for final text.
Keep one core idea per slide and validate rendered PNG dimensions.
docx: Translate visual style into Word typography, margins, heading hierarchy, tables, and callouts.
Keep documents printable unless the user explicitly requests PDF-only visual art direction.
carousel: Use high-resolution fixed canvas, mobile-readable text, and safe margins.
Make each frame complete enough to stand alone.
Reserve a large image or cinematic negative-space zone before adding text.
